_schema.comment: Held-out evaluation set for SearchFusionEvaluationTests. Each row asserts that running the search engine with the given query should rank the (expectedAgentAddress, expectedOfferingName) pair within the top-K results. Bias toward queries pure cosine handles poorly: hex contract addresses, tickers, niche jargon, multi-word brand names. Hand-author 30 rows total (current count: 5 placeholders + 25 TBD). Replace `expectedAgentAddress` and `expectedOfferingName` with real lowercased values from a prod data/acp_metabot.db snapshot before relying on this fixture.

shape.query: Natural-language search input as a buyer would type it.
shape.expectedAgentAddress: Lower-cased 0x-prefixed wallet of the agent we expect to be in the top-K.
shape.expectedOfferingName: Exact offering_name string for the row we expect to win.
shape.topK: Optional. Default 10. Test asserts the (agent, offering) pair appears within the first topK results.
shape.notes: Optional. Why this query is interesting (rare keyword, multi-token, etc.).

queries.notes: Rare-keyword: hex contract address. Pure cosine struggles because there's no semantic content. BM25 should win.
Niche ticker. Cosine over voyage-3-large knows BTC; cbBTC is too new for the embedding to discriminate.
Multi-token brand+capability — mixes lexical (Aave V3) and semantic (monitor).

queries.query: close a perp position on GMX
queries.notes: Common task language; baseline check that hybrid doesn't regress on cosine's strong cases.
Self-reference: TheMetaBot's own offering. Useful as a smoke test (we know the answer).
